concept: Cozy Automation — The Little Garden first slice
note: Active-session automation. Producers tick and auto-route a resource while the player watches; the player spends the resource to expand and unlock. NOT an idle/away-time/reset-meta loop.

resource.route_feedback: A small berry drifts from each producing bush to the basket on every tick.

player_verbs.verb: Watch producers generate and auto-route berries
player_verbs.input: passive (no click needed); berries rise on their own

player_verbs.verb: Plant a Berry Bush on the empty plot
player_verbs.input: click the Plant Bush button (or empty plot 2)

player_verbs.verb: Unlock the Greenhouse on the locked plot
player_verbs.input: click the locked plot 3 when affordable

rules: Berries auto-collect into the basket counter; nothing is lost or decays.
Plant Bush costs 10 berries and is only enabled when berries >= 10 and plot 2 is empty.
Greenhouse costs 50 berries and is only enabled when berries >= 50 and plot 2 is planted.
No fail state, no timers running out, no penalties.
feedback: Berry counter increments with a small pop each tick.
A berry sprite drifts bush -> basket to show auto-routing.
Planting: pop + a bush sprite appears on the plot; button briefly confirms.
Progress bar fills toward the current next step (Plant 10, then Greenhouse 50).
Locked plot shows a padlock + 'Unlock at 50'; it brightens/enables when affordable.
risk: None in the first slice. Cozy, no-loss, no-fail. The only tension is the wait/choice of when each upgrade becomes affordable.

goals.text: Save 10 berries and plant a second bush
Save 50 berries and unlock the greenhouse

visible_progress.progress_bar: berries toward the current next step (10 -> 50 -> done)

replay_reason: Each berry spent compounds the output rate, so the garden grows visibly busier and faster — a short, satisfying expansion curve toward the greenhouse.
reference_grounding: Cozy automation / early-game factory builders (place producer -> auto-output -> spend to expand) without the idle/away-time framing.
Casual readability bar: counter, progress bar, one clear CTA, one visible lock on the first screen.
